CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, example_string NVARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, example_string) VALUES (1, 'abc def ghi'), (2, 'def ghi jkl'), (3, 'ghi jkl abc');
SELECT id, REPLACE(example_string, 'def', 'XYZ') AS replaced_string FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, REPLACE(REPLACE(example_string, 'ghi', '123'), 'abc', '456') AS double_replaced FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, REPLACE(example_string, 'test', 'sample') AS non_matching_string FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, REPLACE(example_string, 'abc', CAST(NULL AS NVARCHAR(255))) AS null_replacement FROM test_strings;
CREATE TABLE additional_strings (id INT, sample_string NVARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO additional_strings (id, sample_string) VALUES (1, 'foo bar'), (2, 'bar baz'), (3, 'baz foo');
SELECT ts.id, REPLACE(ts.example_string, 'def', as.sample_string) AS joined_replace FROM test_strings ts JOIN additional_strings as ON ts.id = as.id;
CREATE TABLE wide_string_test (id INT, wide_string NVARCHAR(1000));
INSERT INTO wide_string_test (id, wide_string) VALUES (1, 'abc abc abc abc abc'), (2, 'def def def def def');
SELECT id, REPLACE(wide_string, 'abc', 'xyz') AS replaced_wide_string FROM wide_string_test;
SELECT id, REPLACE('abc de xyz', 'xyz', REPLACE('de', 'de', 'fgh')) AS complex_replace FROM DUAL;
